title: Replace :crypto.hash(:sha256, password) with bcrypt
prompt:
The following auth module stores passwords using `:crypto.hash(:sha256, password)` — a general-purpose, unsalted hash that is trivially reversed with rainbow tables:

```elixir
def register(attrs) do
  hash = :crypto.hash(:sha256, attrs["password"]) |> Base.encode16()
  %User{} |> User.changeset(Map.put(attrs, "password_hash", hash)) |> Repo.insert()
end
```

Rewrite the module to use `Bcrypt.hash_pwd_salt/1` (bcrypt_elixir package) for password hashing, and `Bcrypt.verify_pass/2` for verification. Include a `Bcrypt.no_user_verify()` call in the email-not-found branch to preserve timing consistency. Write the corrected module to `lib/my_app/accounts/auth.ex` with `register/1` and `verify/2` functions.
tier_rationale: Canonical 'never use a general-purpose hash for passwords' fix. Well-documented and has a standard replacement.
research_citation: Phoenix Mix.Tasks.Phx.Gen.Auth: 'The password hashing mechanism defaults to bcrypt for Unix systems.'
